json.title: German A
json.description:
**IB German A (Literature)** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the analysis and interpretation of German literary works across various genres including novels, poetry, plays, and essays**, with a strong focus on **critical literary analysis, cultural contexts, and the exploration of global issues through literature**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Guided textual analysis of two unseen passages | 2h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay on two studied literary works | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 25% |
| **Higher Level Essay** | Essay on one studied literary text (1200-1500 words) | N/A | External | 20% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | 10-minute prepared response + 5-minute questions on global issue | 15 min | 15 min | 20% |
